name: Prachin Buri (Thailand)
description: A a red field bearing a large golden circular seal, charged at the centre with the seal of Prachin Buri and the province name in Thai script.
history: Used as the official flag of Prachin Buri province by the provincial government (changwat). Most Thai provincial flags follow a common formula: the provincial seal (the same seal used on the provincial coat of arms) charged on a single-colour field. The designs are traditional and date back to the formation of modern provincial administration in Thailand.

features.symbolism: the provincial seal — a heraldic emblem depicting iconography (a Buddhist stupa, sacred mountain, river, or other local landmark) characteristic of the province; see the Wikipedia article for details.
